name: Pixel-Art Product Reveal
description: Hyper-motion product reveal for a small handheld / cult-object gadget that has its own distinct two-tone screen aesthetic — Playdate-class, Game Boy-class, e-ink class. Photoreal hero device (the only color in the frame) sits as a HARD layer-split cutout against a smooth hand-illustrated duotone halftone-dithered world: ink-on-paper / risograph / Mac-System-1 register, NOT chunky 8-bit blocks. 8 cuts averaging ~1.9s, ends on a clean wordmark + tagline lockup over a chroma slab in the brand's signature color. The single physical-interaction gimmick (crank, dial, twist-flap, slider) is the gesture every scene revolves around.

stackSummary.keyframes: openai/gpt-5.4-image-2 via OpenRouter for product fidelity + typography (10/14 stills); google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview for hybrid photoreal-subject + illustrated-world scenes (4/14 stills, only when a hard layer-split between photoreal element and flat duotone world is the headline). Pass 3-4 ATOMIC style refs per call, not one composite screenshot.
stackSummary.i2v: bytedance/seedance-2.0 multi-frame (first+last) is the default — works for everything from entrance slams to photoreal-hand 180-degree orbits. Single-frame for the 2 macro inserts. AVOID kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro multi-frame: still broken on OpenRouter (`400 File is not in a valid base64 format`) as of 2026-05-18 — confirmed across two postmortems. Kling-pro single-frame works but is reserved for tight-portrait expression-only acting; on this template no such shot exists, so seedance owns the whole pipeline.
stackSummary.voiceover: None. This template is music + SFX only. Adding a human VO over a duotone-halftone world breaks the wordless print-zine register.
stackSummary.captions: None in the body. The wordmark + tagline lockup at scene-08 is hand-illustrated typography baked into the keyframe, NOT a Remotion caption overlay.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — generate 5 parallel variants on different chiptune/lo-fi/indie-cinematic axes after the visual cut locks. STRIP ALL BRAND NAMES from the prompt (Game Boy / NES / SNES / Tetris / Mario Kart / Disasterpeace / Anamanaguchi all 400 with `bad_prompt`). Use the 400 response's `prompt_suggestion` field on retry.

constants.compositionWarning: Remotion is project-specific at src/videos/<id>/ — clone scenes.ts/index.tsx skeleton from playdate-pixel-001, do NOT expect a generic composition in src/lib/templates/

variationAxes: outro chroma slab style (full-bleed brand color / centered hero on slab / wordmark over halftone-burst / tagline-only over slab)
hyper-motion archetype (orbit-pan, parallax-strip, halftone-burst, pixel-tile slide-in, focus-rack push)
hook intent (mystery silhouette, gimmick-first close-up, ensemble-cast lineup, environmental wide)
doNotCopyLiterally: The yellow Playdate, the crank gimmick, the named Panic gamecards (Casual Birder / Pick Pack Pup / Hyper Meteor / Inventory Hero), the exact 8-scene beat order, the 'Just for fun.' tagline. Those are the source-project's identity. The TEMPLATE is the framework: photoreal hero device + duotone halftone world + hard layer-split + named-style-ref method + 8-cut hyper-motion + wordmark-on-chroma-slab outro.
referenceNotes: Reference-required gate (AGENTS.md #3) ALWAYS applies — this template only makes sense for a SPECIFIC real product. Before any keyframe gen, the user must supply: (1) a canonical product photo or three-view (front + side + back), and (2) 8-15 ATOMIC style references in the brand's signature illustration register (game cards, manual cutaways, packaging art — one work per file, not a composite screenshot). If the brand has no public illustration corpus, propose pulling one via Playwright on the brand site BEFORE writing the storyboard. AI-improvised branding fails the gate — this template is allergic to it because the whole vibe rests on the brand's already-established two-tone language.

slots.description: Product / device name. Examples: Playdate, Polaroid I-2, Game Boy Color, Stylophone, Brother label-maker.
Manufacturer / brand name (Panic Inc., Polaroid, Nintendo, Dubreq, Brother).
The single chroma allowed in the frame. Examples: Playdate yellow #FFD400, Polaroid white, Game Boy pea-green, Kodak gold #FFB400, deep cyan, magenta, neon-orange.
Plain-English color name (Playdate yellow, Kodak gold, Nothing red). Used in prompts where the hex does not read naturally.
What kind of device: handheld console, e-ink reader, retro camera, mini-synth, label-maker, lockbox, dictation recorder, vintage calculator.
Physical interaction gag: crank, dial, twist-flap, slider, magnetic latch, pull-tab, flip-up screen, side-button-chord.
Verb form of the gimmick: cranks, twists, dials, slides, latches, pulls, flips, chords.
Dark chroma in the duotone palette (charcoal, midnight, ink, sepia). The brand color is the third chroma.
Light chroma in the duotone palette (cream, bone, newsprint, vellum, pea-green).
Plain-language palette descriptor. Examples: charcoal-on-cream, midnight-on-bone, ink-on-newsprint, sepia-on-vellum.
Named style anchors. Examples: risograph zine, Mac System 1, vintage manual cutaway, newspaper-comic-strip, botanical-engraving.
Four mascot characters that orbit the product. Examples: 4 game-card mascots, 4 instrument personifications, 4 vintage-manual icons, 4 weather sprites.
Brand wordmark for the outro slam.
One-line tagline for the outro slam (under or near the wordmark).
Audience descriptor. Examples: gen-z product collectors, indie-handheld enthusiasts, retro-tech archive nerds.
